# vike-sitemap-generator

> Vite plugin for Vike that automatically generates sitemap.xml at build time

vike-sitemap-generator is a Vite build plugin for Vike (a full-stack framework built on Vite). It collects page URLs from Vike's prerender context (SSG) or route config (SSR) and generates standards-compliant sitemap XML during the build.

## Key features

- Automatic URL collection from Vike's SSG prerender context or SSR route patterns
- Per-page metadata via co-located `+sitemap.ts` files using Vike's extension system
- Per-page URL enumeration via `+sitemapUrls.ts` for SSR apps with parameterized routes
- Parallel metadata resolution with configurable concurrency (default 10)
- Automatic sitemap index splitting for large sites exceeding 50,000 URLs
- External sitemap references for multi-app domains (sitemap index)
- Configurable `<lastmod>`, `<priority>`, `<changefreq>`, and `<image:image>` per URL
- Trailing slash control (boolean, per-route rules, or function)
- Exclude paths by exact string, regex, or per-page `exclude: true`
- Optional `robots.txt` integration (auto-append `Sitemap:` directive)
- Dry-run mode for previewing output without writing files
- Configurable output directory independent of Vite's build output
- Built-in `getLastModFromGit` and `getLastModFromGithub` helpers

## Install

```
npm install vike-sitemap-generator
```

Peer dependencies: `vike >= 0.4.0`, `vite >= 7.0.0`

## Quick start

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import vike from "vike/plugin";
import { vikeSitemap } from "vike-sitemap-generator";

export default {
  plugins: [
    vike(),
    vikeSitemap({ baseUrl: "https://example.com" }),
  ],
};
```

## Per-page metadata

Register the extension, then create `+sitemap.ts` files in page directories:

```ts
// pages/+config.ts
import vikeSitemapConfig from "vike-sitemap-generator/config";
export default { extends: [vikeSitemapConfig] };

// pages/about/+sitemap.ts (static config)
export default { priority: 0.8, changefreq: "monthly", lastmod: "2025-06-15" };

// pages/blog/@slug/+sitemap.ts (callback with access to +data.ts)
import type { SitemapPageConfigFn } from "vike-sitemap-generator";
import type { Data } from "./+data";
export default ((context) => ({
  priority: 0.7,
  lastmod: context.data.updatedAt,
  images: context.data.images.map((img) => ({ loc: img.url, title: img.alt })),
})) satisfies SitemapPageConfigFn<Data>;

// pages/admin/+sitemap.ts
export default { exclude: true };
```

Per-page values override global plugin options. For dynamic routes, export a function that receives `SitemapPageContext<Data>` (`{ url, routeParams, data }`) where `data` comes from `+data.ts`.

## URL enumeration with `+sitemapUrls.ts`

For SSR apps with parameterized routes, provide concrete URLs via `+sitemapUrls.ts`:

```ts
// pages/blog/@slug/+sitemapUrls.ts (static list)
export default ["/blog/hello-world", "/blog/second-post"];

// pages/blog/@slug/+sitemapUrls.ts (async function)
export default async () => {
  const posts = await fetch("https://cms.example.com/posts").then((r) => r.json());
  return posts.map((post) => `/blog/${post.slug}`);
};
```

URLs from `+sitemapUrls.ts` are matched against page route patterns, so per-page `+sitemap.ts` config and route params are still resolved.

## Plugin options

| Option              | Type                                          | Default        | Description                                                       |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `baseUrl`           | `string`                                      | (required)     | Base URL (e.g. `"https://example.com"`)                           |
| `outFile`           | `string`                                      | `"sitemap.xml"`| Output filename relative to outDir                                |
| `outDir`            | `string`                                      | Vite's outDir  | Independent output directory                                      |
| `trailingSlash`     | `boolean \| TrailingSlashRule[] \| function`   | `undefined`    | Trailing slash configuration                                      |
| `lastmod`           | `(url: string) => string \| undefined`        | `undefined`    | Async callback for `<lastmod>`                                    |
| `priority`          | `number \| PriorityRule[] \| function`         | `undefined`    | Priority configuration                                            |
| `changefreq`        | `Changefreq \| ChangefreqRule[]`               | `undefined`    | Change frequency configuration                                    |
| `images`            | `(url: string) => SitemapImage[] \| undefined` | `undefined`    | Async callback for `<image:image>`                                |
| `additionalUrls`    | `string[]`                                    | `[]`           | Extra URLs to include                                             |
| `exclude`           | `(string \| RegExp)[]`                         | `[]`           | URL patterns to exclude                                           |
| `concurrency`       | `number`                                      | `10`           | Max concurrent metadata resolutions                               |
| `maxUrlsPerSitemap` | `number`                                      | `50000`        | Threshold for sitemap index splitting                             |
| `robots`            | `boolean`                                     | `false`        | Append `Sitemap:` directive to robots.txt                         |
| `dryRun`            | `boolean`                                     | `false`        | Log XML to console without writing files                          |
| `externalSitemaps`  | `string[]`                                    | `[]`           | External sitemap URLs to include in the sitemap index             |

## SitemapPageConfig (for +sitemap.ts)

| Field        | Type             | Description                                  |
| ------------ | ---------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `priority`   | `number`         | Priority value (0.0 - 1.0)                   |
| `changefreq` | `Changefreq`     | Change frequency                             |
| `lastmod`    | `string`         | ISO 8601 date string                         |
| `images`     | `SitemapImage[]`  | Image entries                                |
| `exclude`    | `boolean`        | Exclude page from sitemap                    |

`+sitemap.ts` can export either a `SitemapPageConfig` object or a `SitemapPageConfigFn` callback.

## SitemapUrlsConfig (for +sitemapUrls.ts)

```ts
type SitemapUrlsConfig = string[] | (() => string[] | Promise<string[]>);
```

`+sitemapUrls.ts` exports either a static array of URL paths or a function (sync/async) that returns them.

## SitemapPageConfigFn (callback for dynamic routes)

```ts
type SitemapPageConfigFn<Data = unknown> = (context: SitemapPageContext<Data>) => SitemapPageConfig | Promise<SitemapPageConfig>;
```

## SitemapPageContext

| Field         | Type                     | Description                                         |
| ------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `url`         | `string`                 | Concrete URL path (e.g. `"/blog/post-1"`)           |
| `routeParams` | `Record<string, string>` | Extracted route params (e.g. `{ slug: "post-1" }`)  |
| `data`        | `Data`                   | Page data from `+data.ts` (SSG only, `unknown` by default) |

## Export paths

- `vike-sitemap-generator` -- main plugin, types, and fetcher helpers
- `vike-sitemap-generator/config` -- Vike extension config for `+sitemap.ts` and `+sitemapUrls.ts` support
